data.epi_content:
"A pistol... Ha. Heh."

I laughed for a long time, holding my stomach.

There was really no need to bring up such a thing.

She truly was a strange girl who couldn't read the situation or the atmosphere properly.

Well, I suppose that sort of thing appears charming to men.

"Purpose? You're asking about its purpose? That's not really something you should be asking about. Ha. Heh."

It's genuinely ridiculous that she would even bring this up.

"Vivian, aren't you laughing?"

"Is this something to laugh about?"

"You barge into someone else's room, invade their bathroom without permission, see them naked, and now you're touching their belongings without asking. How could I not laugh at that? And what, purpose? Are you worried I might shoot someone?"

I threw my coffee cup to the floor.

There wasn't a dramatic shattering sound, just a slight clatter as the cup broke.

Nevertheless, it must have seemed threatening to Vivian, as she flinched.

"Don't worry, at least it won't be you."

Oh no, I might receive divine punishment again.

I slapped her once and my entire family disappeared—what if I get into an argument with her?

I might get struck by lightning from the sky.

"That's right, it's better to laugh than get angry.

When a powerless, stupid girl loses everything, all she can do is laugh."

Without my gun, I'm nothing.

I can't use proper magic, I'm not particularly intelligent, and the only thing I'm good at is running my mouth.

Without my gun, I'm nothing.

Yes, the things I can do.

The only things I can do... mocking, getting irritated, acting like a girl... that's right. I am a girl.

That's right, I was. I always forget.

"I accidentally... truly accidentally dropped the cup. Did I startle you? I'm sorry."

The blessing of firearms is truly remarkable—whether child, woman, or elder, one simply needs to pull the trigger to transform living organic matter into inorganic.

And I threw a bit of sarcasm at the wicked woman who had taken away my blessing.

"I wonder where my pistol is. It's a keepsake from my father.

It would be terribly sad if a thief were to take away one of the few mementos I have left."

Perhaps the word "thief" bothered her.

Vivian, who had seemed lifeless until now, challenged me with a retort.

The atmosphere was so different from before that I involuntarily flinched.

Perhaps Vivian was actually the one who was scared.

She asked with slightly trembling eyes and an anxious voice.

"Th-thief?"

And I explained to her what a thief was.

"Vivian, a thief is someone who steals or takes things that belong to others or aren't their own.

And since someone entered my room without permission and moved my father's last keepsake somewhere else, I think it's fair to say I've been robbed."

Only then did Vivian mumble where she had put the gun.

"Robbed? I just... I just put it on the bed. In case you might..."

When I looked at the bed after hearing that, I saw it had merely been moved aside slightly.

But the important issue was that she had touched someone else's belongings in the first place.

And she had broken down the door and forced her way in without permission.

Vivian trailed off.

Though I already knew what she was going to say, I let out a mocking laugh and pressed her, as if telling her to finish her sentence.

I knew very well that Vivian wouldn't be able to finish what she was saying.

"In case I might what? Haha, in case I might what?"
data.epi_content2:
"......"

"Vivian, didn't you learn anything when you got slapped last time?

That there are certain lines you shouldn't cross with some people?

It's not just about physical boundaries—words have lines too."

I got up from the chair and walked to the bed.

I picked up the pistol.

The ivory grip felt like it stuck perfectly to my hand, making me slightly emotional.

Vivian flinched slightly whenever she saw me holding and handling the gun.

She seemed somewhat afraid, but also half sad and half angry.

"Even when you got close to Evan, even when Evan ignored me and talked about how lovely you were, even when you refused when I subtly suggested you should stay away from Evan—I never once resorted to direct violence.

The only reason I hit you that one time was because you touched me without my permission.

That's what we call a line.

Everyone's standards are different, but well, you'll have to figure that out yourself."

I held the gun in my right hand and spun the barrel with my left.

I didn't look at Vivian, just fiddled with the gold decorations on the gun.

I wasn't particularly interested, but I didn't want to look at Vivian's face.

Still, I should say something.

Whether to pressure her or to drive her away.

"Vivian, why don't you say something?"

"I'm sorry. But it was a situation that could easily be misunderstood..."

What misunderstanding?

Probably, probably I didn't intend to die today.

And I'm not even sure if I would die.

I don't like pain.

"Ah, is this what you meant by 'in case'?"

"...Huh?"

I put the gun to my temple, closed my eyes slightly, then opened them and pulled the trigger.

Click.

Forty percent seems to be a lower probability than I thought.

No bullet came out.

At first, Vivian had a slightly dumbfounded expression, then shock, followed by anger from an unknown source.

Well, just because she's the protagonist doesn't mean she's flawless. Being in a romance novel, she must have some empty spaces in her heart.

Not being able to play with the village children, being ostracized, and witnessing her father's suicide firsthand—isn't that enough trauma?

She had a seizure, her eyes turned bloodshot as she approached me, snatched the gun from my hand and threw it away.

Then she pinned me down.

Being weak and without energy, my wrists were firmly gripped as I was restrained on the bed.

I tried to squirm a little, but it seemed insufficient to overcome a healthy, energetic young girl in her prime.

The fact that my recent diet consisted only of chocolate and coffee probably contributed to that.

Vivian stiffened and stared at me with eyes that seemed almost manic, growling as she spoke.

"Are you crazy?!"

Spit flew onto my face.

I ignored it and answered.

The thought that I might be the only one in this world to see her like this made the corners of my mouth turn up.

"Vivian, I told you. There are lines between people.

Don't speak informally to me, and please release my arms.

Well, I don't have the strength to hit you now anyway. Oh, and please leave my room."

"You... you... don't joke around. Even so..."

"That's right, even so, this doesn't seem right."

Just because I slapped your cheek once, just because I tried to separate you from Evan, everything of mine disappears—how could I accept that?

I just wanted to love.
data.epi_content3:
I had harbored those feelings for so long, and because of that, I had hidden them, telling myself it couldn't be. But after you came, I realized them and tried everything to separate you from Evan.

Haha, even those emotions might have been created by someone else.

What a comedy this is.

The reason my family collapsed, the reason Erica's brother had his head cut off for an absurd reason, the reason this girl is suffering now... and the reason I'm here as you now... it's all...

If I blame someone, I'll get so caught up in it that I won't be able to do anything.

Let's just say it's my fault.

I happened to read that novel then, and I happened to become you.

Actually, I could have become Evan or the Crown Prince, or even a vagrant on the street.

"Words don't accomplish anything anymore.

Yesterday, or two days ago, or was it a week ago? I don't know.

Anyway, the people who used to look behind me and fulfill what I said are now locking me in a locker.

The woman who stole my love is pinning me down, speaking informally and intimidating me."

"But that's not a reason to die... is it?"

Has she regained her senses somewhat, or should I say her rationality?

Vivian's eyes, which had been clouded and slightly hazy until just now, regained their vitality, and she started using formal speech again.

It made things a bit more comfortable for me as the listener.

Though the speaker was still uncomfortable.

"I'll listen to whatever you say, so first, first calm down..."

"Whatever you say" doesn't seem like a good thing for a girl to say.

If I had fallen into this world with my original body and heard those words, I might have asked to touch her chest.

Then I could have brushed it off as a light joke and left.

But now, the only words that came to mind were ones that would hurt Vivian.

"Then Vivian, would you please go out to the terrace right now and jump off?

You don't have to die, just get moderately injured."

"What?"

"Haha, don't pretend you didn't understand."

An expression of slight disbelief, confusion, or perhaps incomprehensible fear of me.

"Why do you think I wouldn't dislike you?

Why, because everyone else at this Academy except me has liked you, shown you favor, and loved you?"

I put the gun back on the table.

I didn't particularly feel the urge to shoot Vivian.

"That can't be true.

Everything that's happened to me is because of you, Vivian.

I might have accepted it if it were just terrible luck, but I've realized it wasn't just bad luck—it was predetermined destruction."

Coffee, where's the coffee?

My throat is a bit dry.

Ah, I broke the cup.

Fortunately, there were two more cups left.

I could break another one.

I poured hot water over the coffee filter, which had already been used and smelled slightly sour, and brewed a cup of coffee.

Then I downed that disgustingly tasteless thing in one gulp.

My mouth felt slightly burned.

"It's all mixed up, you know.

I know this sounds like incoherent nonsense, but what I want to say is just...

I don't know, I hate you."

Vivian was looking at me like I was completely insane.

"Yes, that's what I wanted to say.

I hate not just you, but everything. The world too.

Get out, or I'll bite my tongue and kill myself right now."

"B-but..."

As I stared at her intently, Vivian left the room with a confused face.

I stared in that direction for a long time even after she disappeared.
